info.name: John
info.desc: The Holy Gospel According to John

1:
In the beginning was 
the Word, and 
the Word was with God, and 
the Word was God.
The Word Became Flesh
2: He was in the beginning with God.
3: All things were made through him, and without him was not any thing made that was made.
The Word Became Flesh
4:
In him was life,
 and 
the life was the light of men.
The Word Became Flesh
ch. 5:26; 11:25; 1 John 1:2; 5:11; ch. 8:12; 9:5; 12:46; 1:4 Or was not any thing made. That which has been made was life in him
5: The light shines in the darkness, and the darkness has not overcome it.
6:
There was a man 
sent from God, whose name was 
John.
The Word Became Flesh
ver. 33; ch. 3:28; Mal. 3:1; Matt. 3:1; Mark 1:4; Luke 3:2
7:
He came as a 
witness, to bear witness about the light, 
that all might believe through him.
The Word Became Flesh
8: He was not the light, but came to bear witness about the light.
The Word Became Flesh
ver. 20
9: The true light, which gives light to everyone, was coming into the world.
10:
He was in the world, and the world was made through him, yet 
the world did not know him.
The Word Became Flesh
11:
He came to 
his own,
 and 
his own people
did not receive him.
The Word Became Flesh
ch. 13:1; 1:11 People is implied in Greek; 1:11 Greek to his own things; that is, to his own domain, or to his own people; Matt. 21:38; ch. 5:43; [ch. 3:11, 32]
12:
But to all who did receive him, 
who believed in his name, 
he gave the right 
to become 
children of God,
The Word Became Flesh
1 John 5:1; [Gal. 3:26]; See ch. 11:52; 1 John 3:1; [Matt. 5:45]; See 1 John 5:13
13:
who 
were born, 
not of blood 
nor of the will of the flesh nor of the will of man, but of God.
The Word Became Flesh
14:
And 
the Word 
became flesh and 
dwelt among us, 
and we have seen his glory, glory as of the only Son
 from the Father, full of 
grace and 
truth.
15:
(
John bore witness about him, and cried out, “This was he of whom I said, 
‘He who comes after me ranks before me, because he was before me.’”)
The Word Became Flesh
See ver. 7; ver. 27, 30; See Matt. 3:11
16:
For from 
his fullness we have all received, 
grace upon grace.
The Word Became Flesh
Eph. 1:23; 3:19; 4:13; Col. 1:19; 2:9; [Matt. 25:29]; 1:16 Or grace in place of grace
17: The Word Became Flesh
18:
No one has ever seen God; 
the only God,
 who is at the Father’s side,
he has made him known.
The Testimony of John the Baptist

[Matt. 11:27]; See ch. 3:32; 1:18 Greek in the bosom of the Father; 1:18 Or the only One, who is God; some manuscripts the only Son; ch. 5:37; 6:46; Ex. 33:20; Col. 1:15; 1 Tim. 6:16; 1 John 4:12, 20; [ch. 12:45]; ver. 14; See ch. 3:16
19:
And this is the 
testimony of John, when the Jews sent priests and Levites from Jerusalem to ask him, 
“Who are you?”
The Testimony of John the Baptist
20: He confessed, and did not deny, but confessed, “I am not the Christ.”
The Testimony of John the Baptist
21:
And they asked him, “What then? 
Are you Elijah?” He said, “I am not.” “Are you 
the Prophet?” And he answered, “No.”
The Testimony of John the Baptist
22: So they said to him, “Who are you? We need to give an answer to those who sent us. What do you say about yourself?”
The Testimony of John the Baptist
23:
He said, “I am 
the voice of one crying out in the wilderness, ‘Make straight
 the way of the Lord,’ as the prophet Isaiah said.”
The Testimony of John the Baptist
Cited from Isa. 40:3; See Matt. 3:3; 1:23 Or crying out, ‘In the wilderness make straight
24: (Now they had been sent from the Pharisees.)
The Testimony of John the Baptist
25:
They asked him, 
“Then why are you baptizing, if you are neither the Christ, nor Elijah, nor the Prophet?”
The Testimony of John the Baptist
Matt. 3:6; Mark 1:4; Luke 3:3, 7
26:
John answered them, 
“I baptize with water, but among you stands one you do not know,
The Testimony of John the Baptist
27:
even 
he who comes after me, the strap of whose sandal I am not worthy to untie.”
The Testimony of John the Baptist
28: These things took place in Bethany across the Jordan, where John was baptizing.
Behold, the Lamb of God
29:
The next day he saw Jesus coming toward him, and said, “Behold, 
the Lamb of God, who 
takes away the sin 
of the world!
Behold, the Lamb of God
30:
This is he of whom I said, 
‘After me comes a man who ranks before me, because he was before me.’
Behold, the Lamb of God
31:
I myself did not know him, but 
for this purpose I came baptizing with water, that he might be revealed to Israel.”
32:
And John 
bore witness: 
“I saw the Spirit descend from heaven like a dove, and 
it remained on him.
Behold, the Lamb of God
See ver. 7; [Isa. 11:2; Acts 10:38]; Matt. 3:16; Mark 1:10; Luke 3:22
33:
I myself did not know him, but 
he who sent me to baptize 
with water said to me, ‘He on whom you see the Spirit descend and remain, 
this is he who baptizes 
with the Holy Spirit.’
Behold, the Lamb of God
Matt. 3:11; Mark 1:8; Luke 3:16; Acts 1:5; [ch. 3:5]; ver. 6; Luke 3:2
34:
And I have seen and have borne witness that this is the Son
 of God.”
Jesus Calls the First Disciples
1:34 Some manuscripts the Chosen One
35: The next day again John was standing with two of his disciples,
Jesus Calls the First Disciples
36:
and he looked at Jesus as he walked by and said, “Behold, 
the Lamb of God!”
Jesus Calls the First Disciples
See ver. 29
37: The two disciples heard him say this, and they followed Jesus.
Jesus Calls the First Disciples
38: Jesus turned and saw them following and said to them, “What are you seeking?” And they said to him, “Rabbi” (which means Teacher), “where are you staying?”
Jesus Calls the First Disciples
39: He said to them, “Come and you will see.” So they came and saw where he was staying, and they stayed with him that day, for it was about the tenth hour.
Jesus Calls the First Disciples
1:39 That is, about 4 p.m.
40:
One of the two who heard John speak and followed Jesus
 was Andrew, Simon Peter’s brother.
Jesus Calls the First Disciples
1:40 Greek him; For ver. 40-42, [Matt. 4:18-22; Mark 1:16-20; Luke 5:2-11]
41:
He first found his own brother Simon and said to him, “We have found 
the Messiah” (which means Christ).
Jesus Calls the First Disciples
42: He brought him to Jesus. Jesus looked at him and said, “You are Simon the son of John. You shall be called Cephas” (which means Peter ).
Jesus Calls Philip and Nathanael
1:42 Cephas and Peter are from the word for rock in Aramaic and Greek, respectively; ch. 21:15-17; Matt. 16:18; 1 Cor. 1:12; 3:22
43: The next day Jesus decided to go to Galilee. He found Philip and said to him, “Follow me.”
Jesus Calls Philip and Nathanael
44:
Now 
Philip was from Bethsaida, the city of Andrew and Peter.
Jesus Calls Philip and Nathanael
45:
Philip found 
Nathanael and said to him, “We have found him of whom 
Moses in the Law and also the prophets wrote, Jesus 
of Nazareth, 
the son of Joseph.”
46:
Nathanael said to him, 
“Can anything good come out of Nazareth?” Philip said to him, “Come and see.”
Jesus Calls Philip and Nathanael
47: Jesus saw Nathanael coming toward him and said of him, “Behold, an Israelite indeed, in whom there is no deceit!”
48: Nathanael said to him, “How do you know me?” Jesus answered him, “Before Philip called you, when you were under the fig tree, I saw you.”
49:
Nathanael answered him, 
“Rabbi, 
you are the Son of God! You are the 
King of Israel!”
50: Jesus answered him, “Because I said to you, ‘I saw you under the fig tree,’ do you believe? You will see greater things than these.”
51: And he said to him, “Truly, truly, I say to you, you will see heaven opened, and the angels of God ascending and descending on the Son of Man.”
Ezek. 1:1; Matt. 3:16; Luke 3:21; See Dan. 7:13; [Gen. 28:12]; 1:51 The Greek for you is plural; twice in this verse

1:
On 
the third day there was a wedding at 
Cana in Galilee, and the mother of Jesus was there.
The Wedding at Cana
2:
Jesus also was invited to the wedding with 
his disciples.
3: When the wine ran out, the mother of Jesus said to him, “They have no wine.”
The Wedding at Cana
4: And Jesus said to her, “Woman, what does this have to do with me? My hour has not yet come.”
The Wedding at Cana
5: His mother said to the servants, “Do whatever he tells you.”
6:
Now there were six stone water jars there 
for the Jewish rites of purification, each holding twenty or thirty 
gallons.
The Wedding at Cana
2 Chr. 4:5 (Gk.); 2:6 Greek two or three measures (metrētas); a metrētēs was about 10 gallons or 35 liters; ch. 3:25; [Mark 7:3, 4]
7: Jesus said to the servants, “Fill the jars with water.” And they filled them up to the brim.
The Wedding at Cana
8: And he said to them, “Now draw some out and take it to the master of the feast.” So they took it.
The Wedding at Cana
9:
When the master of the feast tasted 
the water now become wine, and did not know where it came from (though the servants who had drawn the water knew), the master of the feast called the bridegroom
10: and said to him, “Everyone serves the good wine first, and when people have drunk freely, then the poor wine. But you have kept the good wine until now.”
The Wedding at Cana
11:
This, the first of his signs, Jesus did at Cana in Galilee, and manifested 
his glory. And 
his disciples believed in him.
The Wedding at Cana
See ch. 1:14; ver. 2
12:
After this he went down to Capernaum, with his mother and 
his brothers
 and his disciples, and they stayed there for a few days.
Jesus Cleanses the Temple

See Matt. 12:46; 2:12 Or brothers and sisters. In New Testament usage, depending on the context, the plural Greek word adelphoi (translated “brothers”) may refer either to brothers or to brothers and sisters
13:
The Passover of the Jews was at hand, and Jesus 
went up to Jerusalem.
Jesus Cleanses the Temple
14: In the temple he found those who were selling oxen and sheep and pigeons, and the money-changers sitting there.
15: And making a whip of cords, he drove them all out of the temple, with the sheep and oxen. And he poured out the coins of the money-changers and overturned their tables.
Jesus Cleanses the Temple
16: And he told those who sold the pigeons, “Take these things away; do not make my Father’s house a house of trade.”
Jesus Cleanses the Temple
17:
His disciples remembered that it was written, 
“Zeal for your house will consume me.”
Jesus Cleanses the Temple
Cited from Ps. 69:9
18:
So the Jews said to him, 
“What sign do you show us for doing these things?”
Jesus Cleanses the Temple
19: Jesus answered them, “Destroy this temple, and in three days I will raise it up.”
Jesus Cleanses the Temple
20:
The Jews then said, “It has taken forty-six years to build this temple,
 and will you raise it up in three days?”
Jesus Cleanses the Temple
2:20 Or This temple was built forty-six years ago
21:
But he was speaking about 
the temple of his body.
Jesus Cleanses the Temple
22:
When therefore he was raised from the dead, 
his disciples remembered that he had said this, and they believed 
the Scripture and the word that Jesus had spoken.
Jesus Knows What Is in Man
23:
Now when he was in Jerusalem at the Passover Feast, many believed in his name 
when they saw the signs that he was doing.
Jesus Knows What Is in Man
24:
But Jesus 
on his part did not entrust himself to them, because 
he knew all people
Jesus Knows What Is in Man
25:
and needed no one to bear witness about man, for 
he himself knew what was in man.
Jesus Knows What Is in Man
[See ver. 24 above]

1:
Now there was a man of the Pharisees named 
Nicodemus, 
a ruler of the Jews.
You Must Be Born Again
2:
This man came to Jesus
by night and said to him, 
“Rabbi, 
we know that you are a teacher come from God, for no one can do these signs that you do 
unless God is with him.”
3: Jesus answered him, “Truly, truly, I say to you, unless one is born again he cannot see the kingdom of God.”
You Must Be Born Again
See ch. 1:13; 3:3 Or from above; the Greek is purposely ambiguous and can mean both again and from above; also verse 7; ver. 36; [2 Cor. 5:17; Gal. 6:15; 1 Pet. 1:3, 23]
4: Nicodemus said to him, “How can a man be born when he is old? Can he enter a second time into his mother’s womb and be born?”
You Must Be Born Again
5: Jesus answered, “Truly, truly, I say to you, unless one is born of water and the Spirit, he cannot enter the kingdom of God.
6: That which is born of the flesh is flesh, and that which is born of the Spirit is spirit.
You Must Be Born Again
ch. 6:63; 3:6 The same Greek word means both wind and spirit; 1 Cor. 15:50
7: Do not marvel that I said to you, ‘You must be born again.’
You Must Be Born Again
3:7 The Greek for you is plural here; See ver. 3; ch. 5:28
8: The wind blows where it wishes, and you hear its sound, but you do not know where it comes from or where it goes. So it is with everyone who is born of the Spirit.”
You Must Be Born Again
3:8 The same Greek word means both wind and spirit; [Eccles. 11:5; Ezek. 37:9]; 1 Cor. 12:11
9:
Nicodemus said to him, 
“How can these things be?”
10: Jesus answered him, “Are you the teacher of Israel and yet you do not understand these things?
You Must Be Born Again
11: Truly, truly, I say to you, we speak of what we know, and bear witness to what we have seen, but you do not receive our testimony.
You Must Be Born Again
3:11 The Greek for you is plural here; also four times in verse 12; See ver. 32
12: If I have told you earthly things and you do not believe, how can you believe if I tell you heavenly things?
You Must Be Born Again
13: No one has ascended into heaven except he who descended from heaven, the Son of Man.
You Must Be Born Again
ver. 31; ch. 6:38, 42, 62; 1 Cor. 15:47; [Rom. 10:6]; 3:13 Some manuscripts add who is in heaven; Prov. 30:4; [Acts 2:34; Eph. 4:9]; [ch. 7:34]
14: And as Moses lifted up the serpent in the wilderness, so must the Son of Man be lifted up,
15: that whoever believes in him may have eternal life.
For God So Loved the World
3:15 Some interpreters hold that the quotation ends at verse 15; ver. 36; [ch. 15:4; 16:33; 1 John 5:12, 20]
16: “For God so loved the world, that he gave his only Son, that whoever believes in him should not perish but have eternal life.
For God So Loved the World
Rom. 8:32; See ch. 1:29; Rom. 5:8; Eph. 2:4; 2 Thess. 2:16; 1 John 3:1; 4:9, 10; ch. 10:28; 3:16  Or  For this is how God loved the world
17: For God did not send his Son into the world to condemn the world, but in order that the world might be saved through him.
For God So Loved the World
18: Whoever believes in him is not condemned, but whoever does not believe is condemned already, because he has not believed in the name of the only Son of God.
For God So Loved the World
See 1 John 5:13; ch. 5:24; [Mark 16:16]
19: And this is the judgment: the light has come into the world, and people loved the darkness rather than the light because their works were evil.
For God So Loved the World
20: For everyone who does wicked things hates the light and does not come to the light, lest his works should be exposed.
For God So Loved the World
21: But whoever does what is true comes to the light, so that it may be clearly seen that his works have been carried out in God.”
John the Baptist Exalts Christ
22:
After this Jesus and his disciples went into the Judean countryside, and he remained there with them and 
was baptizing.
John the Baptist Exalts Christ
23: John also was baptizing at Aenon near Salim, because water was plentiful there, and people were coming and being baptized
John the Baptist Exalts Christ
24:
(for 
John had not yet been put in prison).
25:
Now a discussion arose between some of John’s disciples and a Jew over 
purification.
John the Baptist Exalts Christ
26:
And they came to John and said to him, 
“Rabbi, he who was with you across the Jordan, 
to whom you bore witness—look, he is baptizing, and 
all are going to him.”
John the Baptist Exalts Christ
See ch. 1:7; ch. 12:19; ver. 2
27:
John answered, 
“A person cannot receive even one thing 
unless it is given him 
from heaven.
John the Baptist Exalts Christ
See Matt. 21:25; 1 Cor. 4:7; Heb. 5:4; ch. 6:65; [James 1:17]
28:
You yourselves bear me witness, that I said, 
‘I am not the Christ, but 
I have been sent before him.’
John the Baptist Exalts Christ
See ch. 1:20; Mal. 3:1; Mark 1:2; Luke 1:17; Acts 19:4
29:
The one who has the bride is the bridegroom. 
The friend of the bridegroom, who stands and hears him, 
rejoices greatly at the bridegroom’s voice. Therefore this joy of mine is now complete.
John the Baptist Exalts Christ
See Matt. 25:1; Matt. 9:15; Judg. 14:20; Song 5:1
30: He must increase, but I must decrease.”
John the Baptist Exalts Christ
3:30 Some interpreters hold that the quotation continues through verse 36; Matt. 3:11
31:
He who comes from above 
is above all. He who is of the earth belongs to the earth and 
speaks in an earthly way. 
He who comes from heaven 
is above all.
Rom. 9:5; Eph. 1:21; [1 John 4:5]; See ver. 13; ch. 8:23
32:
He bears witness to what he has seen and heard, 
yet no one receives his testimony.
John the Baptist Exalts Christ
33:
Whoever receives his testimony 
sets his seal to this, 
that God is true.
John the Baptist Exalts Christ
34:
For he whom 
God has sent utters the words of God, for he gives the Spirit 
without measure.
John the Baptist Exalts Christ
[Ezek. 4:11, 16]; See ver. 17
35:
The Father loves the Son and 
has given all things into his hand.
John the Baptist Exalts Christ
See ch. 5:20; See Matt. 28:18
36: John the Baptist Exalts Christ

1:
Now when Jesus learned that the Pharisees had heard that Jesus was making and 
baptizing more disciples than John
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
2: (although Jesus himself did not baptize, but only his disciples),
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
3:
he left Judea and departed 
again for Galilee.
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
4: And he had to pass through Samaria.
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
5:
So he came to a town of Samaria called Sychar, near the field 
that Jacob had given to his son Joseph.
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
6:
Jacob’s well was there; so Jesus, 
wearied as he was from his journey, was sitting beside the well. It was about the sixth hour.
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
4:6 That is, about noon; ch. 19:28; [Matt. 4:2; 8:24; 21:18]
7: A woman from Samaria came to draw water. Jesus said to her, “Give me a drink.”
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
[See ver. 6 above]
8: (For his disciples had gone away into the city to buy food.)
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
9:
The Samaritan woman said to him, “How is it that you, a Jew, ask for a drink from me, a woman of Samaria?” (
For Jews have no dealings with Samaritans.)
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
10: Jesus answered her, “If you knew the gift of God, and who it is that is saying to you, ‘Give me a drink,’ you would have asked him, and he would have given you living water.”
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
11: The woman said to him, “Sir, you have nothing to draw water with, and the well is deep. Where do you get that living water?
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
12:
Are you greater than our father Jacob? 
He gave us the well and drank from it himself, as did his sons and his livestock.”
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
13: Jesus said to her, “Everyone who drinks of this water will be thirsty again,
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
14: but whoever drinks of the water that I will give him will never be thirsty again. The water that I will give him will become in him a spring of water welling up to eternal life.”
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
15:
The woman said to him, “Sir, 
give me this water, so that I will not be thirsty or have to come here to draw water.”
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
16: Jesus said to her, “Go, call your husband, and come here.”
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
17: The woman answered him, “I have no husband.” Jesus said to her, “You are right in saying, ‘I have no husband’;
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
18: for you have had five husbands, and the one you now have is not your husband. What you have said is true.”
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
19:
The woman said to him, “Sir, I perceive that 
you are 
a prophet.
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
20:
Our fathers worshiped on 
this mountain, but you say that 
in Jerusalem is 
the place where people ought to worship.”
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
21: Jesus said to her, “Woman, believe me, the hour is coming when neither on this mountain nor in Jerusalem will you worship the Father.
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
22: You worship what you do not know; we worship what we know, for salvation is from the Jews.
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
23: But the hour is coming, and is now here, when the true worshipers will worship the Father in spirit and truth, for the Father is seeking such people to worship him.
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
24: God is spirit, and those who worship him must worship in spirit and truth.”
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
25:
The woman said to him, “I know that 
Messiah is coming (he who is called Christ). When he comes, 
he will tell us all things.”
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
See ch. 1:41; Deut. 18:18; [ver. 29]
26: Jesus said to her, “I who speak to you am he.”
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
27:
Just then 
his disciples came back. They marveled that he was talking with a woman, but no one said, “What do you seek?” or, “Why are you talking with her?”
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
ver. 8
28: So the woman left her water jar and went away into town and said to the people,
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
29:
“Come, see a man 
who told me all that I ever did. Can this be the Christ?”
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
30: They went out of the town and were coming to him.
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
31:
Meanwhile the disciples were urging him, saying, 
“Rabbi, eat.”
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
32: But he said to them, “I have food to eat that you do not know about.”
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
33:
So the disciples said to one another, 
“Has anyone brought him something to eat?”
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
34: Jesus said to them, “My food is to do the will of him who sent me and to accomplish his work.
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
35: Do you not say, ‘There are yet four months, then comes the harvest’? Look, I tell you, lift up your eyes, and see that the fields are white for harvest.
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
36: Already the one who reaps is receiving wages and gathering fruit for eternal life, so that sower and reaper may rejoice together.
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
37: For here the saying holds true, ‘One sows and another reaps.’
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
38: I sent you to reap that for which you did not labor. Others have labored, and you have entered into their labor.”
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
39:
Many Samaritans 
from that town believed in him 
because of 
the woman’s testimony, “He told me all that I ever did.”
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
40: So when the Samaritans came to him, they asked him to stay with them, and he stayed there two days.
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
41:
And many more believed 
because of his word.
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
42:
They said to the woman, “It is no longer because of what you said that we believe, for we have heard for ourselves, 
and we know that this is indeed 
the Savior 
of the world.”
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
43:
After 
the two days he departed for Galilee.
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
ver. 40
44:
(For Jesus himself had testified 
that a prophet has no honor in his own hometown.)
Jesus and the Woman of Samaria
See Matt. 13:57
45:
So when he came to Galilee, the Galileans welcomed him, 
having seen all that he had done in Jerusalem at the feast. For 
they too had gone to the feast.
46:
So he came again to 
Cana in Galilee, 
where he had made the water wine. And at Capernaum there was an official whose son was ill.
Jesus Heals an Official’s Son
47:
When this man heard that Jesus 
had come from Judea to Galilee, he went to him and asked him to come down and heal his son, for he was at the point of death.
48: So Jesus said to him, “Unless you see signs and wonders you will not believe.”
ch. 2:18; 6:30; [ch. 20:29]; 4:48 The Greek for you is plural; twice in this verse
49:
The official said to him, “Sir, come down 
before my child dies.”
50: Jesus said to him, “Go; your son will live.” The man believed the word that Jesus spoke to him and went on his way.
51:
As he was going down, his servants
 met him and told him that his son was recovering.
52:
So he asked them the hour when he began to get better, and they said to him, “Yesterday at the seventh hour
 the fever left him.”
53: The father knew that was the hour when Jesus had said to him, “Your son will live.” And he himself believed, and all his household.
54: This was now the second sign that Jesus did when he had come from Judea to Galilee.

1:
After this there was a 
feast of the Jews, and Jesus went up to Jerusalem.
The Healing at the Pool on the Sabbath
See ch. 6:4
2:
Now there is in Jerusalem by 
the Sheep Gate a pool, in Aramaic
 called Bethesda,
 which has five roofed colonnades.
The Healing at the Pool on the Sabbath
5:2 Or Hebrew; Neh. 3:1, 32; 12:39; 5:2 Some manuscripts Bethsaida
3:
In these lay a multitude of invalids—blind, lame, and 
paralyzed.
The Healing at the Pool on the Sabbath

5:3 Some manuscripts insert, wholly or in part, waiting for the moving of the water; 4 for an angel of the Lord went down at certain seasons into the pool, and stirred the water: whoever stepped in first after the stirring of the water was healed of whatever disease he had; Matt. 12:10
5: One man was there who had been an invalid for thirty-eight years.
The Healing at the Pool on the Sabbath
